projects.title: FormoSeanIap Platform
projects.summary: The static-first portfolio platform you are on right now: projects, long-form writing, and podcasts presented through a shared design system with deployment-ready AWS scaffolding.
projects.context: I built it as a durable home for technical work and personal writing, not as a one-off resume site. The emphasis is clear structure, lightweight runtime behavior, and long-term maintainability.
highlights: JSON-driven pages keep site structure simple while still allowing richer content and progressive loading where it matters.
The article pipeline generates metadata, search data, per-article JSON, and RSS feeds from local Markdown sources.
Podcast support uses a small proxy-first approach so new episodes can appear after refresh without rebuilding the whole site.

projects.title: Job Application Toolkit
projects.summary: A monorepo for automating the job application workflow: discover relevant LinkedIn roles, generate tailored materials, track them in Notion, and accelerate application forms with a Chrome extension.
projects.context: The project is split into two tools. job_finder handles scraping, filtering, cover-letter generation, CSV output, and Notion sync. form_filler focuses on semi-automatic application filling with deterministic profile mapping plus AI help for nuanced questions.
highlights: LinkedIn scraping flow with deduplication, clearance filtering, AWS keyword matching, and AI-generated cover letters.
Two-stage workflow: shortlist roles in Notion, then open the links and use the extension for assisted application filling.
AI is used selectively for ambiguous questions while deterministic profile data stays local and predictable.
projects.availability_note: Private while I prepare the repository for a cleaner public release.

projects.title: DocuBloom
projects.summary: A collaborative API documentation product where developers can write readable docs, share mock responses, and keep front-end work moving even when the upstream server is unavailable.
projects.context: DocuBloom treated API documentation as a working product surface rather than static reference text. It combined authoring, testing, collaboration, and deployment concerns into one system.
highlights: Supports API testing, collaborator management, and mock-response URLs for interrupted back-end workflows.
The repository documents system design choices including deployment architecture, database design, and error-handling flow.
Infrastructure and performance constraints were considered early, including a low-cost deployment target and load-testing goals.
